header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Learning & Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people development—through human-centered, evidence-based approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Conduct focus groups and 1:1 interviews with an NGO leadership team to surface decision-making and accountability gaps
Conduct {prog|training needs assessments} with 25+ global {cross|stakeholders} to identify {prog|adult learning gaps} and design 3 new {prog|educational programs}
Develop {prog|facilitator guides, learning materials, and workflows} for 70+ {cross|cross-functional} staff, improving program {prog|knowledge} and reducing onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate {prog|training} webinars and {prog|live educational sessions} for 600+ participants, enhancing {prog|program understanding} and driving a 78% program enrollment rate
Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining program {prog|positioning}, {prog|delivery strategy}, and operations via {data|participant feedback analysis}
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed {prog|L&D} strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal {prog|training} on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and {prog|L&D}
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership {prog|workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Optimized onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized campus recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite executives at top universities to attract talent

jd.meta: Talkiatry Learning & Development Specialist — Remote US — $70-75K — Entry level, 2yr adult learning experience required

changeLog.note: Tailored for Talkiatry Learning & Development Specialist (remote US, entry-level, 2yr adult-learning required, $70-75K). L&D recipe: summary role-noun = 'Learning & Development'. InGenius Set A re-worded to JD vocabulary: 'training needs assessments', 'adult learning gaps', 'facilitator guides, learning materials, and onboarding workflows', 'live educational sessions', 'learning content delivery strategy', 'participant feedback analysis'. ODN Pair A (Upskill/Empower — quantified results). NextGen keeps L&D/training emphasis. Vestas keeps workshop/onboarding angle. Skills: LMS (Canvas, Moodle) moved to front — JD names LMS experience. NO invented authoring tools (Articulate/Storyline/Captivate/Rise 360 NOT added — not Jamie's). MS Office/Data Analysis/PM always present. All canonical metrics preserved: $362K, 300+, 25+, 75%, 600+, 78%, 20+, 10+, 2,000+, 566%. No fabrication.
